promotion_candidates.decision_decision: Do not promote the dInput-only Torch row. The full Torch native backward with dweight/dbias is slower than CUDA, the full Triton atomic prototype is also slower plus has an FP32 gradient-buffer contract mismatch, and the focused Torch dInput-plus-BF16-grads hybrid is much slower than the CUDA full-backward baseline.
decision_evidence: 2026-05-20 target-context refresh: dInput-only Torch native 217.568 us; Triton dInput-only 228.320 us
2026-05-20 target-context refresh: full Torch native backward 416.224 us; Triton atomic FP32-grad backward 363.680 us; CUDA full backward remains about 290 us
2026-05-21 focused target-context hybrid: Torch BackwardDInputNativePlusGrads 1964.480 us with BF16 dweight/dbias
promotion_candidates.decision_note: Torch native dInput-only output-mask row is useful for backward decomposition work but needs dweight/dbias before trainer promotion.
promotion_candidates.promotion_gate: add dweight/dbias accumulation before considering trainer promotion

2026-05-21 raw-pointer LibTorch refresh: cached from_blob full-row parity PASS, CUDA runtime 60.826 us, native CUDA kernel 60.056 us, Torch C++ raw 60.011 us

2026-05-21 correctness-enabled optional refresh: Python Torch 59.981 us, Torch C++ 60.000 us, CUDA runtime 63.555 us; the edge remains operator/profiler evidence until a linked trainer route passes smoke

2026-05-21 standalone trainer-link probe scratch/sm120_rounds/libtorch_trainer_link_20260521/validate_libtorch_trainer_link.log: LibTorch executable without torch_python compile PASS, runtime PASS, and zero/copy from_blob probe PASS

selected_backend_rows.decision_note: Use Torch for Python-side operator comparisons; trainer promotion needs a matching C++ call path and smoke gate.

selected_backend_rows.correctness_evidence_note: No dedicated route-level correctness log is required for this runtime primitive; the timing log and CUDA runtime API checks are the available evidence.
selected_backend_rows.decision_decision: Keep as profiler/runtime evidence only. CUDA runtime remains the fastest current logits-sized memset row, but the current GPT-2 trainer has no logits-sized memset call-site to promote.

selected_backend_rows.decision_decision: Rejected for trainer promotion; focused uncontended timing showed CUDA was faster than Triton for the GPT-2 MLP GELU-forward row.

selected_backend_rows.decision_note: Use as a Triton comparison row until a trainer-callable integration beats the current provider.
Plain CUDA/CUDA-runtime route; eligible for trainer selection subject to correctness and TinyStories smoke evidence.

selection_policy: Fastest observed row per exact suite/kernel/shape. Rows without an existing trainer call path remain reference/operator prototypes until route-specific correctness and TinyStories smoke evidence proves promotion. Repo-local decisions mark refreshed rejects and structurally non-promotable rows inactive without deleting their evidence.
